artifacts.description: Structured evidence table summarizing retrieved pediatric RNS studies, available sex representation fields, and whether sex-specific outcomes were reported.
Brief research log documenting search scope, retrieval outcome, and conservative interpretation of the evidence gap.

claims.notes: Supported by accessible PMC full text.
claims.text: In one retrieved multicenter pediatric RNS cohort, 35 patients were included and 34% were female (12 patients).

claims.notes: This is a narrow evidence-gap claim limited to the retrieved sources and extracted text.
claims.text: Among the retrieved pediatric RNS studies summarized here, sex-specific seizure outcomes or formal sex-difference analyses were not clearly reported in the accessible extracted text.

claims.notes: Conservative synthesis reflecting the absence of reproducibly extracted sex-stratified outcome data.
claims.text: The currently retrieved public evidence is sufficient to describe pediatric RNS cohort size and overall outcomes more confidently than to support claims about sex differences among pediatric RNS patients.

limitations: The retrieved pediatric RNS studies were small retrospective series or pooled literature analyses.
Most retrieved abstracts did not provide sex-stratified outcomes.
Only one retrieved full-text pediatric multicenter study clearly exposed baseline female representation in the accessible text.
No strong claim about sex differences in seizure outcomes, complications, or quality of life among pediatric RNS patients was supportable from the saved evidence artifacts alone.
Some relevant full texts may exist behind publisher access barriers and were not reproducibly extracted here.

sources.label: PMC full text: Real-World Preliminary Experience With Responsive Neurostimulation in Pediatric Epilepsy
sources.notes: Accessible full text. Reports cohort size, baseline female proportion, and overall seizure outcomes, but no clear sex-stratified outcome analysis was extracted.

sources.label: PubMed abstract: Responsive neurostimulation for treatment of pediatric refractory epilepsy: A pooled analysis of the literature
sources.notes: Abstract-level access showed pooled pediatric RNS sample size and overall outcomes, but no sex-specific analysis in the retrieved text.

sources.label: PubMed abstract: Responsive Neurostimulation in Pediatric and Young Adult Patients With Drug-Resistant Focal, Multifocal, and Generalized Epilepsy: A Single-Center Experience
sources.notes: Abstract-level access showed overall responder rate and age range, but no sex-stratified outcomes in the retrieved text.

summary: Publicly accessible pediatric RNS literature was identifiable, but the retrieved studies did not clearly report sex-stratified outcomes or formal analyses of sex differences in response among pediatric RNS patients. One multicenter pediatric RNS cohort reported baseline sex composition, which supports a narrow claim about representation rather than outcomes.
title: Sex differences in pediatric epilepsy for RNS patients
